title: Rounding the Square Root to the Nearest Integer

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a single integer n.
statement.outputFormat: Return the integer nearest to the square root of n.
statement.notes: You may assume that n is non-negative and will fit into an integer.

examples.explanation: Square root of 9 is 3, so the function should return 3.
Square root of 16 is 4, so the function should return 4.
Square root of 20 is approximately 4.472, so the function should return 4 or 5 since it's the closest integer.

hints.text: You need to find the floor of the square root of the given number.

editorial.title: Rounding the Square Root to the Nearest Integer
editorial.intuition: You can use the Math library to calculate the square root of the given number and then round it down to the nearest integer.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach is to calculate the square root of the given number and then use if-else conditions to round it down to the nearest integer.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use the Math library to calculate the square root of the given number and then use the floor function to round it down to the nearest integer.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use the Math library to calculate the square root of the given number and then use the floor function to round it down to the nearest integer.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The floor function will return the nearest integer less than or equal to the given number.
editorial.dryRun: Square root of 9 is 3, so the function should return 3.
commonMistakes: Not considering the decimal part of the square root
